title: Applied Causal Inference
summary: Working through the causal inference literature with implementations in Julia and Python. DoubleML, time-series causal models, instrumental variables.
description_long: Dual-language causal methods codebase with tests and validation architecture. Implements DoubleML, instrumental variables, and time-series-aware variants in both Julia and Python. The intent is reference-quality implementations with explicit validation against synthetic data and known answers.
whats_next: Currently dormant; reference-quality state. Future direction: add additional estimators and integrate with the temporal-validation utilities.

title: double_ml_time_series
summary: A LaTeX manuscript + companion code for temporal partially-linear DML, cross-fitting, HAC inference, and synthetic examples. Web edition in progress — Chapter 1 live.
description_long: Implementation and methodology notes for double machine learning extended to time-series settings. Covers temporal partially-linear DML, cross-fitting protocols that respect time ordering, and HAC-style inference. Synthetic examples illustrate where naive cross-fitting fails and what to do instead.
whats_next: Web edition is porting from the LaTeX manuscript — Chapter 1 (potential outcomes + Frisch–Waugh–Lovell) is live; remaining chapters in progress. The full 10-chapter manuscript ships as a PDF in the repo.

title: TemporalValidation / temporalcv
summary: Temporal validation utilities and methodology lineage. Walk-forward splits, purging, embargo windows, and leakage prevention.
description_long: Utilities for temporally-aware cross-validation: walk-forward splits with explicit gaps, purging adjacent observations, embargo windows, and other leakage-prevention patterns. The methodology lineage notes connect classical temporal CV practice to modern uses in causal and ML pipelines.
whats_next: Reference-quality state. Future: package as installable Python/Julia modules with consistent API.

title: Prompt-injection PoC
summary: Methodology proof-of-concept for detecting prompt-injection attacks against LLMs. Rigorous evaluation with bootstrap CIs on lift over baselines.
description_long: DeBERTa-v3 with LoRA, evaluated against a baseline ladder (heuristic → logistic regression → frozen encoder → fine-tuned) on locked holdout splits with bootstrap confidence intervals on lift. Calibration via temperature scaling; reliability and ECE reported on a model card. Framed explicitly as methodology proof-of-concept, not a production detector.
whats_next: Source repo is private — see the public OOD study and eval-toolkit below. V2 methodology complete; finalizing DECISIONS.md (D1-D29 recorded). Next: archive the PoC and migrate the methodology patterns into the eval-toolkit harness.

title: eval-toolkit
summary: Reusable binary-classification evaluation harness: baseline ladder, bootstrap CIs, calibration, stop-gates against overengineering.
description_long: Pre-v1 evaluation library for binary-classification AI/ML systems. Encodes the methodology used in prompt-injection-detector as reusable components: baseline ladders, bootstrap confidence intervals on metrics and lift, calibration tooling, and explicit stop-gates that fire before adding model complexity.
whats_next: Currently in Round 8 of audit; Decision Y.2 is the stop-gate for cutting v1.0. Next: lock v1.0 API and publish to PyPI.

title: prompt-injection portfolio
summary: Broader pre-alpha field log: ongoing prompt-injection research, attack catalogs, eval scenarios beyond the PoC's scope.
description_long: A pre-alpha companion to the prompt-injection PoC. Wider in scope: tracks attack categories, eval scenarios, OOD probes, and methodology variations that don't fit inside the PoC's locked evaluation protocol. Field-log format — assumes the reader has context.
whats_next: Pre-alpha. Direction: select the most-mature scenarios and migrate them into the eval-toolkit + PoC pipeline.

title: DLAI Study Notes
summary: An online book corpus synthesized from deeplearning.ai short courses (in development — repo public, web deploy pending). Per-course .tex → MDX conversion with an audit-during-conversion quality pass. Searchable, math-rendered, with Anki flashcards.
description_long: In-progress corpus of study notes synthesized from DeepLearning.AI short courses. Each course is converted from LaTeX source through a quality-audit MDX conversion into a searchable, math-rendered, citation-tracked online book. Includes BibTeX bibliography integration, Pagefind search, KaTeX math, and Anki .apkg deck export. Built on book-scaffold-astro. Currently local-only — publishing surface pending.
whats_next: PoC for a broader pattern: 10-20 Gold-tier DLAI courses targeted; additional course-derived note projects from other sources (e.g., Bertsekas RL+Control, ssm-foundations chapters) lined up. Deploy to a public URL is the immediate-next step.

title: rl_and_control — RL + Control Theory guide
summary: RL + Control Theory from fundamentals to convergence. 28-week structured syllabus, Python/JAX/Julia experiments, 135-paper indexed bibliography, 12 method-family dossiers. Companion to ssm-foundations.
description_long: In-progress authored guide bridging classical optimal control (Bellman, DP, MPC, LQR/LQG) with modern reinforcement learning (policy gradient, actor-critic, model-based, MPC-RL hybrids). 28-week syllabus organized into 3 parts: RL Foundations (10 weeks) -> Control Theory (8 weeks) -> Convergence (10 weeks). Includes Python (PyTorch/Gymnasium/SB3), JAX (Brax, differentiable MPC), and Julia (ControlSystems.jl, JuMP MPC) experiment tracks. Bibliography: 135 papers indexed across 18 thematic sections with status tracking and 12 method-family dossiers.
whats_next: Local-only; publishing surface (Astro/MDX web guide) planned, matching the ssm-foundations book-scaffold-astro pattern. Bibliography (rl_and_control/references/paper_index.md) is the data source for the /lab/research-graph/ demo (shipped 2026-05-24, densified 2026-05-25).

external_context.label: Bertsekas — Reinforcement Learning and Optimal Control (2019)

title: ssm-foundations
summary: 17-chapter lens-led foundations book bridging numerical analysis and dynamical systems to modern sequence-model architectures (SSMs, Mamba, S4, Hyena, DeltaNet). Successor to a 21-week research-guide effort. Live and in progress — the foundations chapters are authored; the structured-SSM and later chapters are being written.
description_long: Public foundations book derived from the post-transformers research repo. 17 chapters organized lens-first: continuous-time dynamics → discretization → stability theory → structured state-space models → selective scans (Mamba, S4, S4D) → hybrids. Each chapter pairs prose with JAX and Julia companions, reproducible figures, and Julia stability tests. Built on book-scaffold-astro v4 academic preset; deployed via Cloudflare Workers + Static Assets.
whats_next: Live at https://ssm-foundations.brandon-behring.dev. Foundations chapters authored; actively writing the structured-SSM layer (HiPPO, S4/S4D, Mamba) and the later 'beyond SSMs' chapters (linear attention, DeltaNet, xLSTM/RWKV).

title: book-scaffold-astro
summary: npm-published Astro template for long-form technical books — MDX, Paged.js print, Tufte typography, Pagefind search, KaTeX. The scaffold under every book and guide here.
description_long: Reusable scaffold for searchable, math-rendered long-form books: Astro + MDX content, Paged.js print output, Tufte-style typography, and a Koller–Friedman chapter structure. Published to npm and consumed as a versioned dependency, so consumer books (double_ml_time_series, ssm-foundations, the guides) inherit upgrades by bumping a version. Maintained through a dogfooding loop — each consumer surfaces scaffold gaps that feed back as fixes.
whats_next: A reusable Provenance + audit component (Phase 6a) that surfaces the process-as-artifact trail on every consumer chapter.

title: deploy-workflows
summary: Reusable GitHub Actions workflow for deploying Astro sites to Cloudflare Workers + Static Assets. Every book and guide here pins a tagged version of it.
description_long: A single reusable deploy workflow that consumer repos call with secrets inherited: it runs the build and a Wrangler deploy to Cloudflare Workers Static Assets. Centralizing it means a deploy fix ships once and propagates to every consumer that pins the tag, instead of drifting across per-repo YAML. This site deploys through it.
whats_next: Tagged releases carry optional apt-package and PR-preview inputs; an OIDC-auth migration is queued for a later phase.

title: runpod-deploy
summary: Config-driven RunPod orchestration for reusable GPU project deployments — pod templates and run configs for compute-heavy experiments.
description_long: Infrastructure-as-config for GPU work: parameterized RunPod pod definitions and run configs so training and inference experiments launch reproducibly rather than through ad-hoc console clicks. Built to make the compute side of the research loop repeatable.
whats_next: Active; tightening the config schema and templates as more GPU experiments consume it.

title: guides
summary: Hub repo for a family of interview-prep guides for data science, ML, and AI engineering — destined for guides.brandon-behring.dev.
description_long: Hub for a family of interview-prep guides aimed at data-science, ML-engineering, and AI-engineering readers: a landing page, methodology, and about section, with per-guide companion repos plugged in as subroutes. The architecture keeps each guide independently releasable while sharing one branded shell. The custom domain (guides.brandon-behring.dev) is not yet bound, so the link is to the source repo.
whats_next: Experimentation is the pilot guide; further guides on data fundamentals, ML systems, and AI engineering follow in Phase 1, then the custom domain is bound.

title: guides-experimentation (pilot)
summary: Pilot guide under the guides hub: experimentation methodology — A/B testing, sample size and power, variance reduction, sequential testing.
description_long: The first companion guide for the hub: a practitioner treatment of experimentation — design, sample-size and power, variance reduction, sequential testing, and decision-making under cost asymmetry. Currently schema plus skeleton; the full chapter build lands in Phase 1.
whats_next: Full multi-chapter MDX build in Phase 1, then deploys under the hub's /experimentation/ subroute.

title: Agentic Coding: Principles and Practices
summary: Long-form Astro/MDX book on agentic coding — principles, patterns, and system design for AI-assisted development. Built on book-scaffold-astro.
description_long: An authored book on agentic coding — principles, patterns, and system design for AI-assisted development — written as a research artifact (decision log, bibliography) on top of the book-scaffold-astro template. It also serves as the scaffold's reference consumer.
whats_next: Content build toward a v1.0; deploys to its own subdomain when ready and inherits the Provenance component.

title: mathematical-guides (family)
summary: A family of formal, Definition–Theorem–Proof guides for a mathematician audience — a hub plus per-subject siblings (transformers, reinforcement learning). Private while in development.
description_long: Formal guides written in a Definition–Theorem–Proof register for readers who want the mathematics first. A hub coordinates per-subject sibling guides — transformers and reinforcement learning so far — each a standalone Astro/MDX book deploying under the hub. The repositories are private while the structure and first chapters are built, so there's no public link yet.
whats_next: Deploy-configured for a mathematical.brandon-behring.dev subdomain (target still being confirmed); goes public as chapters reach a shippable state.

title: claude-books
summary: A planned handbook on building with Claude Code — the successor that retires the earlier LaTeX claude-best-practices draft. Private; pre-v1.
description_long: A planned multi-part handbook on AI-assisted development with Claude Code. It is the deliberate successor to an earlier LaTeX draft: the LaTeX repos are treated as drafts and archived once their successor ships v1.0. It's listed here to make that sunset path explicit rather than leaving the old draft looking like the live artifact.
whats_next: Ships publicly when the handbook reaches v1.0; the LaTeX predecessor is archived at that point.

title: annuity-pricing
summary: Python library for annuity pricing and valuation — bond math, option Greeks, and Monte Carlo for fixed and variable annuity products.
description_long: A Python library for pricing and valuing annuity products: the bond-math foundation, Black–Scholes option Greeks, and Monte Carlo simulation for embedded guarantees. A companion Julia stack (AnnuityCore.jl, AnnuityProducts.jl, AnnuityData.jl) reimplements the core engine for performance and multiple dispatch. This reflects earlier domain work and is deliberately not the site's lead.
whats_next: Dormant at reference quality; the companion Julia stack is slated for registry publication in a later phase.

title: insurance-ai-toolkit
summary: AI/ML toolkit for insurance work — VM-21-style reserve calculation and lapse-rate modeling.
description_long: A toolkit applying machine learning to actuarial problems: VM-21-style variable-annuity reserve calculation, stochastic lapse and mortality modeling, and policy-level risk assessment. It pairs with annuity-pricing for end-to-end valuation workflows.
whats_next: Active development on the lapse models and reserve backtesting.

title: research-kb
summary: Hybrid-search research knowledge base — BM25 + vector + citation graph over 478 sources / 236K chunks, exposed via MCP. The system powering the citation-graph demo here.
description_long: A hybrid-search knowledge base I built to accelerate my own research: BM25 full-text, vector embeddings, and a citation and concept graph over 478 sources (236K chunks), focused on causal inference. It's exposed as MCP tools so an assistant can search and traverse it directly, and it's the data source behind the /lab/research-graph demo on this site. Infrastructure for my own learning, not a product.
whats_next: Expanding corpus coverage beyond causal inference (time-series, econometrics) and densifying the citation graph.

external_context.label: Live demo: RL + Control citation graph

title: research-agent
summary: LangGraph research agent on top of research-kb — structured synthesis with citation validation.
description_long: A multi-agent research pipeline built on research-kb: it decomposes a question, retrieves evidence, synthesizes a structured answer, and validates citations against the sources. It's the querying layer of the same enabling system — how I turn the knowledge base into vetted answers.
whats_next: Tightening synthesis quality and wiring outputs back into research-kb for cross-topic reuse.

title: research_toolkit
summary: Claude Code skills for systematic research workflows — literature review, assumption audits, cross-domain concept mapping — built on research-kb + research-agent.
description_long: A collection of Claude Code skills exposing the research-kb and research-agent stack as repeatable workflows: literature review, causal-assumption audits, citation-network analysis, and cross-domain concept discovery. The point is to validate research claims systematically before anything is published.
whats_next: A methodology page on how that validation works — the local research-* dogfooding sandboxes that vet claims before they ship — is coming under this cluster.

title: prompt-injection detection: OOD generalization study
summary: Methodology case study asking whether prompt-injection detectors trained on direct injection still work on attack families they never saw. Paper-shaped, with deployed docs.
description_long: A companion study to the prompt-injection PoC with a different question: do detectors generalize out-of-distribution? Detectors trained on direct-injection attacks are evaluated against unseen attack families (indirect, optimization-based, context-poisoning). The artifact is paper-shaped — an IMRAD write-up plus a narrative — with CI-checked reproduction and deployed docs. It is a sibling of the existing prompt-injection detector, not a replacement.
whats_next: Findings feed back into the eval-toolkit harness as out-of-distribution robustness test patterns.
